Strategic support agreements beyond initial aircraft sales strengthen geopolitical ties while creating long-term revenue streams for defense manufacturers through training and sustainment contracts.

Wizz Air's Wi-Fi investment signals that ultra-low-cost carriers are now competing on service amenities, not just price, reshaping budget airline economics.

Regulatory Mar 25, 2026

House Moves to Bring Back Civil Overland Supersonic Flight

Measure would direct FAA to write new regulations for civil aircraft flying faster than Mach 1.

Regulatory reform could unlock a $100B+ market for supersonic business jets if FAA successfully balances sonic boom restrictions with operational viability.

Make-Or-Break Week: United Airlines Enters Final Contract Talks With Flight Attendants

Representatives of the carrier's cabin crew are hoping to be able to lock down a tentative agreement.

Successful contract resolution could set wage and benefit precedent for other major carriers' upcoming labor negotiations.

Drones Mar 25, 2026

DJI just nuked drone prices across the board

Something strange is happening to DJI drone prices right now. And if you’ve ever even briefly considered buying a drone, this is one of those moments where ignoring it might come back to haunt you. Because this isn’t a typical seasonal sale or a small round of discounts tucked away on a product...

DJI's aggressive price reduction strategy signals potential market consolidation pressure, creating a limited-window opportunity for operators to upgrade fleets at historically low acquisition costs.

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

Air Arabia debuts Panasonic Avionics multi-orbit IFC

Already a customer of Panasonic Avionics’ eXW wireless inflight entertainment system, UAE-based Air Arabia is now flying with the IFEC giant’s Ku-band multi-orbit onboard connectivity solution, Runway Girl Network can reveal. The all-Airbus A320 Family operator is among a cluster of carriers...

Air Arabia's adoption of Panasonic's multi-orbit Ku-band connectivity enables more reliable IFC across diverse flight routes by combining satellite coverage instead of relying on single-orbit systems.

See Runway Girl Network →

FAA Letter Says Whiteman Airport Obligated to Stay Open

Los Angeles County still continues to study closure.

FAA's obligatory-use language constrains airport closure options despite local political pressure, establishing federal precedent for general aviation facility preservation.

Supply chain disruptions from geopolitical tensions are forcing carriers to reduce capacity and pre-emptively secure fuel inventory, signaling potential cascading effects across Asian aviation networks.

Southwest Airlines Cabin Reconfiguration Increases Seat Pitch, Reducing Overall Passenger Capacity

Southwest Airlines has added extra-legroom seats to its fleet.

Southwest's capacity reduction through reconfiguration signals a strategic pivot toward premium seating and higher per-seat revenue despite lower overall throughput.

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

$15.8 Billion Vote Of Confidence: Airbus Scores Massive 101-Plane Deal

The European planemaker continues to make big steps forward in a key market.

Airbus's 101-plane order signals sustained confidence in narrow-body demand despite economic headwinds, reinforcing its competitive position against Boeing in a recovering global aviation market.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

Why Can’t The Airbus A350 Be Powered By Any Other Engine Type?

The Airbus A350 and Rolls-Royce Trent XWB are a match made in aviation heaven.

The A350's airframe design was engineered exclusively around the Trent XWB's specifications, making retrofit with alternative engines economically and structurally infeasible unlike previous aircraft platforms.

See Simple Flying →

Safety Mar 25, 2026

JetBlue Flight Halted After Bizarre Runway Encounter With Coyote

The aircraft did not suffer any damage and was back in the air shortly after the incident, transporting passengers to their original destination.

Wildlife incursions at airports pose unexpected operational disruptions that can delay flights despite minimal physical damage, highlighting the need for enhanced perimeter wildlife management.

See Simple Flying →

Drones Mar 25, 2026

Warehouse drones are quietly transforming alcohol distribution across America

In a move that shows just how fast warehouse automation is evolving, Corvus Robotics has teamed up with Southern Glazer’s Wine & Spirits to scale autonomous inventory drones across one of the largest beverage distribution networks in the US. more…

Autonomous warehouse drones are enabling rapid fulfillment at scale for heavily regulated industries, signaling broader adoption of autonomous systems beyond tech-focused sectors.

Safety Mar 25, 2026

RNO Mustang 9 Departure

Pilots departing from runways 17 left and right at Reno-Tahoe International need to pay special attention to the Mustang 9 Departure. It is extremely important that the procedure on the Mustang 9...

The Mustang 9 departure at RNO requires heightened pilot attention due to specific procedural complexities that differ from standard departure operations.

Drones Mar 25, 2026

Bay Area next as Wing expands drone delivery network

The Wing team has announced plans to expand its ultra-fast residential drone delivery service across the San Francisco Bay Area in the coming months, marking a full-circle moment for the company that was originally born inside Google X back in 2012. more…

Wing's Bay Area expansion signals growing regulatory acceptance of autonomous delivery drones in dense urban markets, potentially accelerating FAA approval pathways for competitors.

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

14+ Hours Nonstop: Delta Air Lines Reveals Brand New Long-Haul Route

Delta Air Lines has disclosed another new long-haul route: Los Angeles to Manila. While it has not been officially announced yet, it was revealed in the airline's response to Philippine Airlines' and its application to the US Department of Transportation to begin flights from Manila to Chicago...

Delta's LA-Manila route signals aggressive expansion into underserved Asia-Pacific markets, potentially forcing competitors to accelerate their own long-haul network strategies.

See Simple Flying →

Air Cargo Mar 25, 2026

Meet The New King Of Cargo: Atlas Air Orders 20 Airbus A350Fs

Well, it looks like in a few years’ time, the United States will have ANOTHER A350-operator alongside Delta… but it won’t be flying passengers! That’s because Airbus announced on March 16th that Atlas Air Worldwide has placed a firm order for 20 Airbus A350F freighters, making it the largest...

Atlas Air's 20 A350F order signals a strategic shift toward wide-body freighters for long-haul cargo, potentially reshaping competitive dynamics in the air-cargo market against traditional narrowbody operators.

Porter to launch 12 routes out of new Montreal Metropolitan Airport terminal

Porter Airlines will be the launch carrier at Montreal Metropolitan Airport (YHU) brand new terminal. Starting on June… The post Porter to launch 12 routes out of new Montreal Metropolitan Airport terminal appeared first on AeroTime.

Porter's hub expansion into Montreal's new airport diversifies its regional network beyond Toronto, potentially challenging Air Canada's dominance in Eastern Canadian markets.

Safety Mar 25, 2026

Flying in the Unforgiving Wire Environment (The Rotorcraft Collective)

Many pilots mistakenly believe they will have sufficient reaction time if they encounter power lines and other wires. Yet wire strikes have been among the top three causes of fatal helicopter...

Wire strikes remain a leading fatal helicopter hazard because pilots systematically underestimate closure speeds and overestimate their reaction capability in low-altitude operations.

Military Mar 25, 2026

Photos of A-10s Used to Strike Iranian Naval Vessels Show Video Game-Inspired Nicknames

Images shared by U.S. Central Command suggest that at least some A-10s taking part in Operation Epic Fury carry new video game-inspired nicknames, an operationally irrelevant detail but one likely to interest spotters and modelers. As we already reported a few days ago, the U.S. Air Force A-10...

A-10 Warthog aircraft engaged in recent Middle East operations display unconventional video game-inspired nose art, reflecting how modern military pilots personalize their aircraft amid high-tempo combat missions.

Industry Mar 25, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization across three aviation domains—airlines, airports, and flight operations—offers the most immediate pathway to handle projected traffic growth without proportional environmental burden.

See MDPI →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

Press Release: Immfly, Wizz Air, and gateretail partner on digital cabin

Immfly, Wizz Air, and gateretail have launched an integrated digital cabin ecosystem that unifies onboard retail, passenger and crew connectivity, and operational data into a single platform, marking a significant step forward in the digital transformation of Europe’s commercial aviation sector....

Integrating retail, connectivity, and operations into one platform enables airlines to capture new revenue streams while improving crew efficiency and passenger experience simultaneously.

Military Mar 25, 2026

DOT&E Says No Combat-Capable TR-3-Configured F-35s Were Delivered in FY2025

The latest Director, Operational Test & Evaluation report says no combat-capable F-35 TR-3 aircraft have been delivered to the U.S. military until September 2025 because of the truncated software. The recently released 2025 Director, Operational Test & Evaluation (DOT&E) annual report brought...

F-35 TR-3 software delays continue blocking combat-capable deliveries, extending a critical readiness gap into operational squadrons.

Drones Mar 25, 2026

America’s new 911 drone is always ready

Drones responding to 911 calls are no longer experimental. Across the US, police departments are already using Drone as First Responder (DFR) programs to get eyes on emergencies faster than patrol units. In many cities, drones arrive in under two minutes, well before officers. But even with that...

DFR programs are operationalizing drones as the primary emergency response asset, fundamentally shifting response time expectations from minutes to sub-two-minute arrivals.

See DroneDJ →
